Title: Product brochure review
Description: Verify that the supplier provided a brochure or website that commercially describes the LPFR product submitted for approval.
Preconditions: Supplier approval package is available.
InputOrTrigger: Product brochure or website.
Steps: Review the brochure or website; confirm product illustration exists for hardware products; confirm functionality summary exists; compare the marketed product with the product submitted for approval.
ExpectedResult: A brochure exists and describes the same LPFR product that is the subject of approval.
Notes: Derived from LPFR manual section "Dokumentacija o proizvodu", item 1.

Title: LPFR user manual coverage review
Description: Verify that the LPFR user manual covers all relevant functional, operational, and integration topics required by the tax authority.
Preconditions: Supplier approval package is available.
InputOrTrigger: LPFR user manual.
Steps: Review the user manual; confirm supplier contact data; confirm product configuration and security warnings; confirm system and operational parameter setup; confirm invoice issuance guidance; confirm peripheral usage guidance; confirm error-message guidance; confirm ESIR connection guidance; confirm secure-element installation and configuration guidance.
ExpectedResult: The user manual covers the minimum mandatory topics defined in the manual.
Notes: The LPFR manual explicitly lists the minimum content expected by the authority.

Title: LPFR installation manual review
Description: Verify that installation guidance is sufficient for technical setup of the LPFR product.
Preconditions: Supplier approval package is available.
InputOrTrigger: Installation manual or installation section inside the user manual.
Steps: Review installation instructions; confirm required tools and prerequisites are listed; confirm the sequence of installation steps is described; confirm warnings about product or operating-system damage and installer safety are present.
ExpectedResult: Installation guidance is present and covers the minimum authority-defined installation content.
Notes: The source allows this content to be part of the main user manual.

Title: LPFR visual identification review
Description: Verify that the physical or device-visible product identification matches approval documentation and self-assessment answers.
Preconditions: The LPFR product is physically available for review.
InputOrTrigger: Visual inspection of the LPFR product.
Steps: Check whether manufacturer name, serial number, and hardware and/or software version are visibly available on the product; compare those values with the approval documentation and self-assessment answers.
ExpectedResult: Visible product-identification values exist and match the submitted approval material.
Notes: This is the dedicated visual-review section of the LPFR manual.

Title: Sign a newly received fiscalization request with internet access
Description: Verify the baseline happy path where the LPFR signs a new invoice while internet access is available and the secure element can later receive Proof of Audit.
Preconditions: Approved EFU system with stable internet access; official smart-card security element with valid certificate.
InputOrTrigger: Create a new invoice through the LPFR workflow.
Steps: Insert the smart card; enter a valid PIN; sign the invoice through the LPFR; wait about five minutes for SUF to verify the signed invoice.
ExpectedResult: The operator receives Proof of Audit from the SUF system.
Notes: The source focuses on the operational proof-of-audit behavior rather than the exact transport payload.

Title: Online audit-limit overrun block
Description: Verify LPFR behavior when a signed invoice pushes the secure element over the no-proof-of-audit amount limit while internet access exists.
Preconditions: Approved EFU system with stable internet access; official smart-card security element with valid certificate; current amount is still below the no-proof-of-audit limit.
InputOrTrigger: Issue an invoice whose amount crosses the secure-element audit limit.
Steps: Insert the smart card; enter a valid PIN; issue an invoice that crosses the allowed no-proof-of-audit limit; verify that the LPFR shows a visible audit warning; immediately try to issue a second invoice; wait about five minutes for SUF to send Proof of Audit; try issuing a new invoice again.
ExpectedResult: The first invoice signs successfully and eventually gets Proof of Audit; the immediate second invoice is blocked until the proof arrives; issuance resumes after the limit reset.

Title: Offline audit-limit overrun block
Description: Verify LPFR behavior when a signed invoice crosses the audit limit while the device is offline.
Preconditions: Approved EFU system; internet connection unavailable; official smart-card security element with valid certificate; current amount is still below the no-proof-of-audit limit.
InputOrTrigger: Issue an invoice whose amount crosses the secure-element audit limit while offline.
Steps: Insert the smart card; enter a valid PIN; issue an invoice that crosses the limit; verify a visible audit warning is shown; try to issue another invoice immediately.
ExpectedResult: The first invoice is signed without Proof of Audit; the next invoice is blocked until the offline audit export and proof workflow completes.
Notes: The manual explicitly instructs the reviewer to continue with the remaining offline-audit steps in sequence after this test.

Title: Export audit files to external storage
Description: Verify that audit files can be exported from the LPFR to external media after the limit is reached offline.
Preconditions: The secure element has reached the limit, or the previous offline overrun test has just been executed.
InputOrTrigger: USB flash drive or SD card inserted into the LPFR.
Steps: Insert the USB flash drive or SD card; start copying audit files; observe the in-progress notification; observe the completion notification.
ExpectedResult: All audit files are successfully exported to external storage regardless of whether the smart card remains connected and regardless of which security element signed the invoices.
Notes: The source uses "audit files" to mean both audit packages and the `.arp` file.

Title: Verify exported audit-file structure
Description: Verify that exported audit files use the folder and naming structure expected by the Tax Authority upload portal.
Preconditions: The previous export test completed successfully.
InputOrTrigger: Inspect the contents of the external storage unit.
Steps: Let the LPFR start local audit when external media is inserted, or start it manually on software LPFR variants; verify that a folder named after the security-element JID exists; verify audit packages follow the naming convention `{JID}-{JID}-{integer}.json`; verify that the folder may also contain a `{UID}.arp` file when a smart-card security element was used.
ExpectedResult: Audit files are present in the expected folder structure and naming format so that the tax-authority portal can recognize them.
Notes: This row captures the only source-text naming rules for local audit package export.

Title: Upload audit files to the Tax Authority portal
Description: Verify the manual portal upload step for exported audit files.
Preconditions: The exported audit files were verified structurally in the previous test.
InputOrTrigger: Portal upload of exported audit files.
Steps: Log in to the Fiscalization Administration Portal; open the audit-package upload section; select the correct folder and upload all audit files from external storage; confirm upload completion.
ExpectedResult: All audit files are successfully uploaded.
Notes: The source describes the tax-officer perspective, but the same logical workflow is also relevant for supplier-operated channels.

Title: Download LPFR commands after upload
Description: Verify the manual retrieval of LPFR commands, including Proof of Audit, after audit-package upload.
Preconditions: The previous audit-file upload test completed successfully.
InputOrTrigger: Download LPFR commands for the same security-element JID.
Steps: Log in to the Fiscalization Administration Portal; open the LPFR command download section; enter the same security-element JID used during export verification; complete the portal workflow; download the commands; open the external-storage file named after that JID; copy the downloaded commands into that file.
ExpectedResult: All commands are successfully downloaded from the tax-authority system to external storage.
Notes: This is the bridge between portal-side audit processing and LPFR-side command execution.

Title: Upload downloaded commands back to the LPFR
Description: Verify that the LPFR recognizes and executes downloaded commands from external storage.
Preconditions: The previous command-download test completed successfully.
InputOrTrigger: Insert external storage that now contains the downloaded commands.
Steps: Insert the USB flash drive or SD card with commands into the LPFR; verify that the LPFR recognizes and accepts new commands; execute the commands manually or automatically; wait for the LPFR to report that the commands finished and that the LPFR is operational again.
ExpectedResult: The LPFR executes the new commands successfully and can resume issuing invoices.
Notes: This step operationally clears the offline block caused by the no-proof-of-audit limit.

Title: Verify command-execution results file creation
Description: Verify that the LPFR writes back a command-execution results file after processing downloaded commands.
Preconditions: The LPFR has executed new commands from external storage.
InputOrTrigger: Inspect the LPFR output on the external storage unit.
Steps: Open the folder named after the same security-element JID; verify that a results file named `{JID}.results` was created there.
ExpectedResult: A results file exists in the correct folder with the correct naming convention.
Notes: This is the only explicit source for the `{JID}.results` naming convention.

Title: Upload command-execution results
Description: Verify the final portal upload step for LPFR command-execution results.
Preconditions: The previous results-file test completed successfully.
InputOrTrigger: Portal upload of the `.results` file.
Steps: Log in to the Fiscalization Administration Portal; insert the SD card or USB drive containing the results file; open the LPFR command-results upload section; upload the results file.
ExpectedResult: The results file is uploaded to the SUF system.
Notes: This closes the manual local-audit workflow described by the authority.

Title: Get last signed invoice
Description: Verify that the LPFR can replay the last signed invoice for a given request identifier.
Preconditions: Official smart-card security element with valid certificate; approved EFU system with stable internet access.
InputOrTrigger: Issue any invoice and then call Get Last Signed Invoice with the same request identifier.
Steps: Insert the smart card; enter a valid PIN; issue an invoice with any fiscalization request identifier; execute Get Last Signed Invoice using that identifier; compare the returned invoice with the invoice signed in the earlier step.
ExpectedResult: The LPFR displays or returns the last signed invoice matching the earlier request.
Notes: This is the only explicit manual test for the `GetLastSignedInvoice` contract.

Title: Issue invoices while local audit is running
Description: Verify that local audit does not block invoice issuance.
Preconditions: Official smart-card security element with valid certificate.
InputOrTrigger: Start local audit while the LPFR continues operating.
Steps: Insert the smart card; enter a valid PIN; issue one invoice; start local audit; verify the LPFR shows a local-audit notification; issue another invoice during audit processing.
ExpectedResult: The second invoice is issued successfully while local audit is still running.

Title: Issue Normal Sale invoice
Description: Verify that the LPFR can fiscalize the standard Normal Sale receipt variant and that the result can be verified through the public receipt-verification flow and the journal.
Preconditions: Official smart-card security element with valid certificate.
InputOrTrigger: Issue a `Normal` + `Sale` invoice through Postman, Swagger, or an equivalent client.
Steps: Insert the smart card; enter a valid PIN; issue a Normal Sale invoice; verify the receipt through the QR code or verification hyperlink; inspect the displayed journal.
ExpectedResult: The invoice is issued successfully and the resulting receipt is verifiable.
Notes: Original Serbian title: `Izdavanje racuna Promet Prodaja`.

Title: Issue Normal Refund invoice
Description: Verify that the LPFR can fiscalize the standard Normal Refund receipt variant and that the result is verifiable.
Preconditions: Official smart-card security element with valid certificate.
InputOrTrigger: Issue a `Normal` + `Refund` invoice through Postman, Swagger, or an equivalent client.
Steps: Insert the smart card; enter a valid PIN; issue a Normal Refund invoice; verify the receipt through the QR code or verification hyperlink; inspect the displayed journal.
ExpectedResult: The invoice is issued successfully and the resulting receipt is verifiable.
Notes: Original Serbian title: `Izdavanje racuna Promet Refundacija`.

InputOrTrigger: Issue an `Advance` + `Sale` invoice through Postman, Swagger, or an equivalent client.
Steps: Insert the smart card; enter a valid PIN; issue an Advance Sale invoice; verify the receipt through the QR code or verification hyperlink; inspect the displayed journal.
ExpectedResult: The invoice is issued successfully and the resulting receipt is verifiable.
Notes: Original Serbian title: `Izdavanje racuna Avans Prodaja`.

Title: Issue Advance Refund invoice
Description: Verify that the LPFR can fiscalize the Advance Refund receipt variant and return a verifiable result.
Preconditions: Official smart-card security element with valid certificate.
InputOrTrigger: Issue an `Advance` + `Refund` invoice through Postman, Swagger, or an equivalent client.
Steps: Insert the smart card; enter a valid PIN; issue an Advance Refund invoice; verify the receipt through the QR code or verification hyperlink; inspect the displayed journal.
ExpectedResult: The invoice is issued successfully and the resulting receipt is verifiable.
Notes: Original Serbian title: `Izdavanje racuna Avans Refundacija`.

Title: Issue Training Sale invoice
Description: Verify that the LPFR can issue the Training Sale fiscal-document variant and that the result is verifiable.

Steps: Insert the smart card; enter a valid PIN; issue a Training Sale document; verify the result through the QR code or verification hyperlink; inspect the displayed journal.
ExpectedResult: The Training Sale document is issued successfully and the resulting output is verifiable.
Notes: Original Serbian title: `Izdavanje racuna Obuka Prodaja`.

Description: Verify that the LPFR can issue the Training Refund fiscal-document variant and that the result is verifiable.

Title: Issue Copy Sale invoice
Description: Verify that the LPFR can issue the Copy Sale fiscal-document variant and that the result is verifiable.

ExpectedResult: The Copy Sale document is issued successfully and the resulting output is verifiable.
Notes: Original Serbian title: `Izdavanje racuna Kopija Prodaja`.

Title: Issue Copy Refund invoice
Description: Verify that the LPFR can issue the Copy Refund fiscal-document variant and that the result is verifiable.
Preconditions: Official smart-card security element with valid certificate.
InputOrTrigger: Issue a `Copy` + `Refund` invoice through Postman, Swagger, or an equivalent client.

Title: Issue ProForma Sale invoice
Description: Verify that the LPFR can issue the ProForma Sale fiscal-document variant and that the result is verifiable.
Preconditions: Official smart-card security element with valid certificate.
InputOrTrigger: Issue a `ProForma` + `Sale` invoice through Postman, Swagger, or an equivalent client.
Steps: Insert the smart card; enter a valid PIN; issue a ProForma Sale document; verify the result through the QR code or verification hyperlink; inspect the displayed journal.
ExpectedResult: The ProForma Sale document is issued successfully and the resulting output is verifiable.
Notes: Original Serbian title: `Izdavanje racuna Predracun Prodaja`.

Description: Verify that the LPFR can issue the ProForma Refund fiscal-document variant and that the result is verifiable.
Preconditions: Official smart-card security element with valid certificate.
InputOrTrigger: Issue a `ProForma` + `Refund` invoice through Postman, Swagger, or an equivalent client.
Steps: Insert the smart card; enter a valid PIN; issue a ProForma Refund document; verify the result through the QR code or verification hyperlink; inspect the displayed journal.
ExpectedResult: The ProForma Refund document is issued successfully and the resulting output is verifiable.
Notes: Original Serbian title: `Izdavanje racuna Predracun Refundacija`.

Title: Stop operation after smart-card removal
Description: Verify that removing the smart-card security element during operation prevents further invoice issuance.
Preconditions: Official smart-card security element with valid certificate.
InputOrTrigger: Remove the smart card after entering a valid PIN.
Steps: Insert the smart card; enter a valid PIN; remove the card; check whether the LPFR remains operational.
ExpectedResult: The LPFR must stop operating after the card is removed.

Title: Require PIN again after card reinsertion
Description: Verify that reinserting a previously removed card does not preserve the cached PIN state.
Preconditions: Official smart-card security element with valid certificate.
InputOrTrigger: Remove the card after entering a valid PIN, then reinsert it and try to issue a receipt without a new PIN.
Steps: Insert the smart card; enter a valid PIN; remove the card; reinsert the card; attempt to issue an invoice.
ExpectedResult: Invoice issuance is not possible until a valid PIN is entered again.
Notes: This is the manual extension of the PIN-caching rule.

Title: Check LPFR status endpoint
Description: Verify that the LPFR exposes the correct operational status through the Get Status command.
Preconditions: Official smart-card security element with valid certificate; approved EFU system with stable internet access.
InputOrTrigger: Call `GET /api/v3/status`, potentially followed by PIN entry, and call it again.
Steps: Insert the smart card; execute Get Status; enter a valid PIN if prompted; execute Get Status again; inspect returned status codes.
ExpectedResult: The LPFR returns the correct status code set for its operational state.
Notes: The manual does not prescribe a single code sequence here, but the later degraded-state suites do.

Title: Probe LPFR availability with Attention
Description: Verify the LPFR availability probe endpoint.
Preconditions: Approved EFU system with stable internet access.
InputOrTrigger: Call `GET /api/v3/attention`.
Steps: Start the LPFR; execute the Attention command through Postman, Swagger, or equivalent tooling.
ExpectedResult: The LPFR returns HTTP 200.

Title: No-card environment-parameters failure
Description: Verify the environment-parameters endpoint when a reader is selected but no smart card is inserted.
Preconditions: Application installed; card reader selected; smart card not inserted.
InputOrTrigger: Call `GET /api/v3/environment-parameters`.
Steps: Send the request with the selected reader and no smart card present.
ExpectedResult: HTTP 400 with body `1300` (`Smart Card is not present`).

Title: No-card status degraded-state matrix
Description: Verify the status payload when a reader is selected but no smart card is inserted.
Preconditions: Application installed; card reader selected; smart card not inserted.
InputOrTrigger: Call `GET /api/v3/status`.
Steps: Send the request with the selected reader and no smart card present.
ExpectedResult: HTTP 200 with general status codes `1300`, `1500`, and `0210`.
Notes: This is the clearest manual example of a degraded state returning HTTP 200.

Title: No-card VerifyPin failure
Description: Verify the PIN endpoint when a reader is selected but no smart card is inserted.
Preconditions: Application installed; card reader selected; smart card not inserted.
InputOrTrigger: Call `POST /api/v3/pin`.
Steps: Send the VerifyPin request with the selected reader and no smart card present.
ExpectedResult: HTTP 200 with body `1300` (`Smart Card is not present`).
Notes: This scenario is also reflected in `errors_statuses.csv`.

Title: No-card CreateInvoice failure
Description: Verify invoice creation when a reader is selected but no smart card is inserted.
Preconditions: Application installed; card reader selected; smart card not inserted.
InputOrTrigger: Call `POST /api/v3/invoices`.
Steps: Send any invoice request with the selected reader and no smart card present.
ExpectedResult: HTTP 400 with body `1300` (`Smart Card is not present`).
Notes: This scenario is also reflected in `errors_statuses.csv`.

Title: No-card invoice-by-id failure
Description: Verify the behavior of the invoice lookup endpoint when a reader is selected but no smart card is inserted.
Preconditions: Application installed; card reader selected; smart card not inserted.
InputOrTrigger: Call `GET /api/v3/invoices/2222`.
Steps: Send the invoice lookup request with the selected reader and no smart card present.
ExpectedResult: HTTP 400 with body `1300` (`Smart Card is not present`).
Notes: The source documents this as a standalone invoice-lookup failure case when no smart card is inserted.

Title: Selected-reader CreateInvoice sample request 1
Description: Exercise the first official CreateInvoice request sample shown in the LPFR manual when the application is installed, a card reader is selected, and a card is inserted.
Preconditions: Application installed; card reader selected; smart card inserted.
InputOrTrigger: POST `{"invoiceType":0,"transactionType":"Sale","payment":[{"amount":150,"paymentType":0}],"items":[{"name":"Network Cable","quantity":1,"unitPrice":100,"labels":["B"],"totalAmount":100}]}` to `/api/v3/invoices`.
Steps: Send the sample request exactly as shown in the manual.
ExpectedResult: The source PDF contains an expected-result screenshot on page 23, but the local environment could not extract that image into machine-readable text without guessing.

Title: Selected-reader CreateInvoice sample request 2
Description: Exercise the second official CreateInvoice request sample shown in the LPFR manual when the application is installed, a card reader is selected, and a card is inserted.
Preconditions: Application installed; card reader selected; smart card inserted.
InputOrTrigger: POST the manual's second sample payload with buyer fields, options, GTIN, and Unicode content to `/api/v3/invoices`.
Steps: Send the sample request exactly as shown in the manual.
ExpectedResult: The source PDF contains an expected-result screenshot on page 25, but the local environment could not extract that image into machine-readable text without guessing.

Title: Status when card is inserted but PIN was not verified
Description: Verify status behavior with a reader inserted, a smart card inserted, and no valid PIN entered yet.
Preconditions: Application installed; card reader inserted; smart card inserted; valid PIN not yet entered.
InputOrTrigger: Call `GET /api/v3/status`.
Steps: Send the request before successful PIN verification.
ExpectedResult: HTTP 200 with general status codes `1500` and `0210`.
Notes: This is the manual degraded-state PIN-required matrix.

Title: CreateInvoice when PIN is required
Description: Verify invoice creation before PIN verification succeeds.
Preconditions: Application installed; card reader inserted; smart card inserted; valid PIN not yet entered.
InputOrTrigger: Call `POST /api/v3/invoices`.
Steps: Send any invoice request before PIN verification succeeds.
ExpectedResult: The request fails with code `1500` (`Pin Code Required`).
Notes: `errors_statuses.csv` marks the HTTP status as unresolved, but the manual clearly states the body-level outcome.

Title: Wrong PIN rejection
Description: Verify wrong-PIN handling.
Preconditions: Application installed; card reader inserted; smart card inserted.
InputOrTrigger: Call `POST /api/v3/pin` with an invalid PIN.
Steps: Send a wrong PIN to the VerifyPin endpoint.
ExpectedResult: HTTP 423 with body `2100` (`Pin Not OK`).
Notes: This is one of the cleanest contract-level negative tests in the LPFR manual.

Title: VerifyPin success after PIN entry
Description: Verify successful PIN validation after the correct PIN is entered.
Preconditions: Application installed; card reader inserted; smart card inserted; correct PIN available.
InputOrTrigger: Call `POST /api/v3/pin` with the correct PIN.
Steps: Send a correct PIN to the VerifyPin endpoint.
ExpectedResult: HTTP 200 with body `0100` (`Pin OK`).
Notes: The manual labels the current and expected outcome the same way.

Title: Status after successful PIN verification
Description: Verify status behavior after PIN verification succeeds.
Preconditions: Application installed; card reader inserted; smart card inserted; PIN already verified successfully.
InputOrTrigger: Call `GET /api/v3/status`.
Steps: Send the status request after successful PIN verification.
ExpectedResult: HTTP 200 with body or `gsc` containing `0100` (`Pin OK`).
Notes: The manual gives this as the clean post-authentication steady state.

Title: Product brochure review
Description: Verify that the supplier provided a brochure or website that commercially describes the ESIR product submitted for approval.
Preconditions: Supplier approval package is available.
InputOrTrigger: Product brochure or website.
Steps: Review the brochure or website; confirm product illustration exists for hardware products; confirm functionality summary exists; compare the marketed product with the product submitted for approval.
ExpectedResult: A brochure exists and describes the same ESIR product that is the subject of approval.
Notes: Derived from ESIR manual section `Korisnicka dokumentacija`, item 1.

Title: ESIR user manual coverage review
Description: Verify that the ESIR user manual covers all authority-required product, security, issuance, and error-handling topics.
Preconditions: Supplier approval package is available.
InputOrTrigger: ESIR user manual.
Steps: Review the user manual; confirm supplier contact data; confirm software and hardware version labeling where applicable; confirm configuration and security warnings; confirm system-parameter setup; confirm receipt-issuance guidance; confirm peripheral guidance; confirm error-message guidance.
ExpectedResult: The user manual covers the minimum mandatory ESIR topics defined by the authority.
Notes: The source also notes exceptions for non-transferable approvals and self-service devices.

Title: ESIR installation manual review
Description: Verify that installation guidance is sufficient for technical setup of the ESIR product.
Preconditions: Supplier approval package is available.
InputOrTrigger: Installation manual or installation section inside the user manual.
Steps: Review installation instructions; confirm required tools and prerequisites are listed; confirm the sequence of installation steps is described; confirm damage and safety warnings are present.
ExpectedResult: Installation guidance is present and covers the minimum authority-defined installation content.
Notes: The source allows this content to be merged into the main user manual.

Title: ESIR configuration manual review
Description: Verify that the supplier documented ESIR configuration, including secure-element setup and PFR connection.
Preconditions: Supplier approval package is available.
InputOrTrigger: Configuration manual or configuration section inside the user manual.
Steps: Review configuration guidance; confirm BE or certificate installation guidance; confirm operational-parameter setup; confirm LPFR and/or VPFR connection guidance.
ExpectedResult: Configuration guidance exists and covers the minimum required configuration topics.
Notes: The source allows this content to be merged into the main user manual.

Title: Require PFR data before issuing receipts
Description: Verify that an ESIR cannot issue any receipt unless it receives required data from the connected PFR.
Preconditions: ESIR is active and connected to a PFR; a security element is present; all PFR preconditions are met; a valid printer exists for physical receipts.
InputOrTrigger: Issue a receipt once while connected, then disconnect the LPFR or disable the VPFR certificate and try again.
Steps: Issue a receipt; verify that the receipt type is visible and matches the Administration Portal; remove the BE or physically disconnect the LPFR from the ESIR, or disable the VPFR certificate; try issuing another receipt.
ExpectedResult: Receipt issuance succeeds only while the ESIR is correctly connected to the PFR; once disconnected, the ESIR must refuse issuance and show a notification.
Notes: This is the main operational dependency test for ESIR-to-PFR communication.

Title: Preserve all mandatory PFR data on the rendered receipt
Description: Verify that the ESIR does not omit or alter any mandatory fiscal data returned by the PFR and that the rendered receipt stays aligned with the Administration Portal and journal.
Preconditions: BE present; ESIR active and connected to a PFR; printer installed if the receipt is physical.
InputOrTrigger: Issue any receipt and perform a full visual reconciliation with the Administration Portal and journal.
Steps: Issue a receipt; verify header data; verify cashier identification; verify buyer ID and optional buyer field when applicable; verify the ESIR number format; verify reference number and reference time when applicable; verify receipt type and transaction type; verify item list, amounts, tax items, payment method, PFR time, PFR number, invoice counter, QR size and scanability, frame lines, and the non-fiscal notice on Copy, Training, and ProForma documents.
ExpectedResult: All mandatory fiscal data is present on the receipt and matches the Administration Portal and electronic journal; non-fiscal documents carry the required notice; QR remains scannable.
Notes: This row is intentionally broad because the manual defines it as a full receipt-rendering reconciliation checklist rather than a single-field assertion.

Title: Accept command input when the ESIR supports it
Description: Verify optional command-input support on ESIR devices that expose a command-input channel.
Preconditions: A connected or integrated input device exists for communicating with the ESIR.
InputOrTrigger: Random user commands.
Steps: Enter random commands; observe device behavior.
ExpectedResult: The ESIR responds correctly to all input commands given by the user.
Notes: The source marks this test as optional.

Title: Authenticate with the PFR on every startup
Description: Verify that the ESIR authenticates with the LPFR or VPFR each time it starts.
Preconditions: Started ESIR connected either to an LPFR or to a VPFR over the internet.
InputOrTrigger: Startup and first PFR-authentication sequence.
Steps: Start the ESIR; connect to the PFR; use the ESIR-held BE or LPFR smart card and enter valid credentials such as PIN or PAC; manually establish the ESIR to LPFR connection if needed; wait for the ESIR to confirm that the PFR connection is established.
ExpectedResult: The ESIR authenticates with the PFR on startup and does not complete startup until that connection is available.

Title: Remove items before receipt issuance
Description: Verify optional item-removal support before a receipt is finalized.
Preconditions: ESIR connected to a PFR.
InputOrTrigger: Add items to a pending receipt and remove some of them before issuance.
Steps: Start receipt issuance; add items into the input fields; remove one or more items but keep at least one; complete issuance; visually inspect the issued receipt.
ExpectedResult: The final receipt contains only the items that were not removed.
Notes: The source marks this test as optional.

Title: Apply discounts to items
Description: Verify that discounts can be applied to receipt items and that the final reduced price is what the PFR records.
Preconditions: ESIR connected to a PFR.
InputOrTrigger: Issue a receipt containing at least one discounted item.
Steps: Issue a receipt with at least one discounted item; visually confirm that the discount appears on the receipt; verify that the PFR recorded the final reduced item price.
ExpectedResult: The receipt is issued successfully with the selected discounted item values.
Notes: This is the main manual test for discount propagation into the fiscalized result.

Title: Support every payment method defined by the technical guide
Description: Verify that the ESIR supports each payment method defined by the technical guide.
Preconditions: ESIR connected to a PFR.
InputOrTrigger: Issue one receipt for each supported payment method.
Steps: Issue receipts using each payment method defined by the technical guide; visually confirm that each receipt shows the corresponding payment method.
ExpectedResult: Each payment method can be issued and is rendered correctly on the receipt.

Title: Support multiple payment methods on one receipt
Description: Verify that the ESIR supports mixed and repeated payment methods within a single receipt.
Preconditions: ESIR connected to a PFR.
InputOrTrigger: Issue a receipt that uses multiple payment methods, including one repeated method.
Steps: Issue a receipt with multiple payment methods defined by the technical guide; ensure at least one payment method is repeated on the same receipt; visually inspect the rendered result.
ExpectedResult: The receipt is issued successfully and all payment methods appear correctly.
Notes: This is the explicit manual test for mixed-payment rendering.

Title: Support GTIN on receipt items
Description: Verify that the ESIR accepts GTIN input on items and that GTIN remains visible in the Administration Portal.
Preconditions: ESIR connected to a PFR; item data includes a GTIN.
InputOrTrigger: Issue a receipt containing at least one item with GTIN.
Steps: Issue a receipt with at least one GTIN-bearing item; inspect the Administration Portal and confirm the GTIN is visible; verify that GTIN-bearing items remain visible in the receipt item-name area as required by the source.
ExpectedResult: The receipt is issued successfully and GTIN-bearing items remain traceable in the portal output.
Notes: The source says GTIN need not be printed directly but must remain visible in portal-side review.

Title: Require a reference number for Refund and Copy receipts
Description: Verify that the ESIR requires and renders the correct reference number for Refund and Copy receipts.
Preconditions: ESIR connected to a PFR; a prior Normal or Advance Sale receipt exists for reference.
InputOrTrigger: Issue a Refund receipt and then a Copy receipt using the original receipt reference.
Steps: Issue a Refund or Copy receipt with the correct original reference number entered manually or automatically; visually inspect the rendered reference number; repeat the workflow for the Copy variant.
ExpectedResult: Refund and Copy receipts are issued successfully and contain the correct reference number.
Notes: The manual notes that this case is also covered again in the receipt-issuance section.

Title: Allow a reference number on Normal Sale when closing an advance refund
Description: Verify the reference-number use case where a Normal Sale closes a prior Advance Refund flow.
Preconditions: ESIR connected to a PFR; a prior Advance Refund receipt exists.
InputOrTrigger: Issue a Normal Sale that closes the prior advance flow and references the original document.
Steps: Issue a Normal Sale receipt with the correct original reference number for the earlier Advance Refund, entered manually or automatically; visually inspect the rendered reference number.
ExpectedResult: The final Normal Sale receipt is issued successfully and contains the correct reference number.
Notes: The Serbian text says this is the `Promet Prodaja` case that closes an advance.

Title: List and search prior receipts
Description: Verify optional electronic-journal listing and search support.
Preconditions: Functional ESIR.
InputOrTrigger: Electronic-journal search.
Steps: Find the option for searching previously issued receipts; execute a search; validate the returned data against the Administration Portal.
ExpectedResult: The ESIR shows prior receipts and can successfully search for a specific one.
Notes: The source marks this test as optional.

Title: Create a new item in the ESIR catalog
Description: Verify catalog support for creating a new product or service item.
Preconditions: Functional ESIR.
InputOrTrigger: Item creation in the local ESIR catalog.
Steps: Create a new item and save it; open the list of existing items; confirm the new item appears.
ExpectedResult: A new catalog item is created successfully.
Notes: This is a pure ESIR-side catalog-management test.

Title: Select item quantity with three decimals
Description: Verify that the ESIR supports fractional quantities with three decimal places.
Preconditions: ESIR connected to a PFR; BE present.
InputOrTrigger: Issue a new receipt with an item quantity expressed using three decimals.
Steps: Issue a new receipt; choose an item quantity using three decimal places; visually inspect the printed or displayed quantity.
ExpectedResult: The receipt is issued successfully and the item quantity matches the selected fractional value.
Notes: This is the main manual quantity-precision test.

Title: Change an existing item price
Description: Verify catalog support for modifying and persisting an existing item price.
Preconditions: Functional ESIR.
InputOrTrigger: Item-price update in the local ESIR catalog.
Steps: Open the list of available items; change the price of an existing item and save it; reload the item list; reopen the changed item and inspect the saved price.
ExpectedResult: The modified item price is saved successfully.
Notes: This is catalog-level behavior rather than fiscalization behavior.

Title: Add items to a receipt by name and by GTIN
Description: Verify that items can be added to a receipt by filtering their names and by scanning or entering GTIN.
Preconditions: ESIR connected to a PFR; BE present; barcode scanner available if GTIN is scanned.
InputOrTrigger: Add one item by name and another by GTIN to the same receipt.
Steps: Start receipt issuance; filter existing items by name and add the desired item; add another item via GTIN; complete issuance; inspect the final receipt.
ExpectedResult: The receipt is issued successfully and includes both added items.
Notes: The source explicitly says GTIN-based addition is mandatory.

Title: Round item prices and totals correctly
Description: Verify price and total rounding to two decimals using the half-up examples given by the authority.
Preconditions: ESIR connected to a PFR.
InputOrTrigger: Issue a receipt that exercises one-decimal, upward-rounding, downward-rounding, and total-rounding cases.
Steps: Start receipt issuance; add item A priced at 5.1; add item B priced at 10.267; add item C priced at 20.143; add two more items whose quantity-price multiplication produces one total above the 0.005 threshold and one below it; complete issuance; inspect rounded values.
ExpectedResult: Item A is displayed as 5.10, item B as 10.27, item C as 20.14, and the example totals round exactly as prescribed.

Title: Print current tax rates
Description: Verify that the ESIR prints the currently active tax rates from PFR configuration or SUF-sourced parameters.
Preconditions: ESIR connected to a PFR; BE present.
InputOrTrigger: Create an item that uses all available tax labels and issue a receipt with it.
Steps: Create an item that uses all available tax labels; issue a receipt; compare the tax labels and values on the receipt with the Administration Portal data.
ExpectedResult: The receipt is issued successfully and all displayed tax labels and values are current and correct.
Notes: This is the manual tax-rate freshness test.

Title: Print tax labels with tax kind and amount
Description: Verify that the ESIR renders tax labels showing both tax kind and tax amount.
Preconditions: ESIR connected to a PFR; BE present.
InputOrTrigger: Issue a receipt with an item carrying a chosen tax label.
Steps: Create an item with a chosen tax label; issue a receipt; inspect the rendered tax labels.
ExpectedResult: The receipt is issued successfully and the tax labels are present and correct.
Notes: The manual gives explicit examples such as A=20%, B=10%, C=0%, D=0.25%.

Title: Display active taxes and tax rates on command
Description: Verify the ESIR command that displays all active taxes and tax rates.
Preconditions: ESIR connected to a PFR.
InputOrTrigger: Command for displaying active tax rates.
Steps: Run the command that displays active taxes and tax rates; verify that tax rates are shown with two decimals using the same rounding method as the price-rounding test; compare the displayed data with the Administration Portal.
ExpectedResult: The ESIR displays all active taxes and tax rates correctly.
Notes: The source treats this as an ESIR command rather than an invoice-issuance flow.

Title: Reject non-existent tax rates during issuance
Description: Verify that the ESIR and/or PFR prevents receipt issuance when a user enters a tax rate that does not exist.
Preconditions: ESIR connected to a PFR; BE present.
InputOrTrigger: Begin receipt issuance and manually enter a random or non-existent tax-rate value.
Steps: Start receipt issuance; when selecting the tax rate, enter a random value for the rate name or code; try to issue the receipt.
ExpectedResult: The system blocks receipt issuance and returns a notification that the tax rate does not exist.

Title: Deliver receipts electronically without losing fiscal data
Description: Verify that the ESIR can deliver every supported receipt type electronically while preserving mandatory fiscal content.
Preconditions: ESIR connected to a PFR; BE present; a valid electronic communication channel exists, such as email, SMS, or a compatible messaging application.
InputOrTrigger: Issue a receipt and send it through each supported electronic delivery channel.
Steps: Issue a receipt; send it to a valid communication channel; confirm successful delivery and valid receipt form; repeat using all supported communication channels; additionally perform the mandatory-data preservation checks from the broader rendering test.
ExpectedResult: The receipt is issued successfully, delivered through the selected channel, and still contains all mandatory fiscal data.
Notes: This is the manual electronic-delivery parity test.

Title: Receipt sample bundle coverage review
Description: Verify that the supplied receipt sample set covers the minimum authority-defined example bundle before detailed page-by-page review.
Preconditions: ESIR connected to a PFR; sample receipt set prepared for review.
InputOrTrigger: Review the whole sample bundle submitted for approval.
Steps: Check that the sample set contains five items per receipt where required; multiple tax labels; decimal-price cases for rounding verification; user-documentation verification material; minimum requirements; VPFR and LPFR issuance coverage; payment-method coverage; and change handling.
ExpectedResult: The approval sample bundle covers the minimum scenario set expected by the authority.
Notes: This row normalizes the page-20/21 bundle requirements before the page-by-page sample reviews below.

Title: Normal Sale with buyer identification sample review
Description: Review the official sample image for a Normal Sale receipt that contains buyer identification.
Preconditions: ESIR connected to a PFR; BE present; sample data prepared.
InputOrTrigger: Issue a Normal Sale receipt with buyer identification.
Steps: Generate the sample receipt; compare the rendered output against the official PDF image; verify Administration Portal and journal alignment.
ExpectedResult: The rendered receipt matches the official sample for the variant and preserves the mandatory fiscal data.
Notes: The source page is image-only in the current extraction, so exact field overlay must be reviewed in the PDF.

Steps: Generate the sample document; compare the rendered output against the official PDF image; verify Administration Portal and journal alignment.
ExpectedResult: The rendered document matches the official sample for the variant and preserves the mandatory fiscal data.

Description: Review the official sample image for an Advance Sale receipt that contains buyer identification.

Title: Assign a unique SDC invoice number to every Normal Sale invoice
Description: Verify that every Normal Sale invoice receives a unique SDC Invoice No in the RequestedBy UID-SignedBy UID-OrdinalNumber form.
Preconditions: The invoicing system can issue multiple Normal Sale invoices.
InputOrTrigger: Issue at least two Normal Sale invoices.
Steps: Issue a Normal Sale invoice; capture the assigned SDC Invoice No; issue another Normal Sale invoice; compare the values and their structure.
ExpectedResult: Each Normal Sale invoice receives a unique SDC Invoice No composed as RequestedBy UID-SignedBy UID-OrdinalNumber.
Notes: Directly extracted from the generic Test Cases section.

Title: Use the original Sale invoice number when issuing a Refund
Description: Verify that a Refund references the SDC Invoice No of the previous Sale invoice in the Ref No field.
Preconditions: A Sale invoice already exists.
InputOrTrigger: Issue a Refund for a previous Sale.
Steps: Locate a previous Sale invoice; start a Refund flow; populate or inspect the Ref No field; compare it with the original Sale invoice number.
ExpectedResult: The Refund uses the SDC Invoice No of the previous Sale invoice in the Ref No field.
Notes: Directly extracted from the generic Test Cases section.

Title: Use the original Normal receipt number when issuing a Copy
Description: Verify that a Copy receipt uses the Normal Sale or Normal Refund SDC invoice number in the Ref No field.
Preconditions: A Normal Sale or Normal Refund receipt already exists.
InputOrTrigger: Issue a Copy receipt.
Steps: Locate the original Normal receipt; start the Copy flow; populate or inspect the Ref No field; compare it with the original NS or NR invoice number.
ExpectedResult: The Copy receipt uses the original NS or NR SDC Invoice No in the Ref No field.
Notes: Directly extracted from the generic Test Cases section.

Title: Issue a fiscal invoice end to end
Description: Verify the generic invoice-issuance flow from cashier input through signed receipt delivery.
Preconditions: The invoicing system is operational and connected to the required SDC endpoint.
InputOrTrigger: Cashier creates and completes an invoice.
Steps: Select an invoice type; register the transaction by typing, selecting, or scanning items; choose the payment method; finish the invoice; send the invoice data to V-SDC or E-SDC; receive the invoice response; deliver the receipt to the customer.
ExpectedResult: The final result is a digitally signed fiscal receipt that can be printed or shared, whose internal data is stored in TaxCore, with a valid QR code or verification URL and a receipt counter in the a/b IL form.
Notes: The source explicitly labels this as Issue Invoice and provides steps plus expected result.

Title: Issue invoices with buyer identification across invoice and transaction types
Description: Verify that buyer identification is supported across invoice types and transaction types, with Buyer TIN requested at the start of invoice creation and Buyer Cost Center optional.
Preconditions: The invoicing system supports buyer identification.
InputOrTrigger: Start invoice creation for a buyer-identified transaction.
Steps: Begin invoice creation; ask the buyer for the Buyer TIN; optionally capture the Buyer Cost Center; continue the issuance flow for the chosen invoice and transaction type.
ExpectedResult: Invoices with buyer identification can be issued in all invoice and transaction types, with Buyer TIN collected at the start and Buyer Cost Center optional.
Notes: The source states the rule directly but does not enumerate per-type substeps.

Title: Provide ESIR equipment required for manual testing
Description: Verify that the applicant can provide the ESIR device and mandatory supporting material needed if the Tax Authority performs additional manual testing.
Preconditions: An ESIR product is being submitted for approval.
InputOrTrigger: Administrative or technical review asks whether manual-testing material can be delivered.
Steps: Confirm availability of an ESIR device prepared for testing; confirm all mandatory hardware components and installation cables are available; confirm compatible receipt paper can be delivered with the device.
ExpectedResult: The applicant can deliver the ESIR device, the required hardware and cables, and receipt paper compatible with the ESIR device for manual testing.
Notes: Extracted from the self-assessment section P1-P3 under Potrebno za rucno testiranje.

Title: Provide an LPFR sample and supporting material required for manual testing
Description: Verify that the applicant can provide an LPFR sample and the supporting material required for detailed manual evaluation.
Preconditions: An LPFR product is being submitted for approval.
InputOrTrigger: Administrative or technical review asks whether manual-testing material can be delivered.
Steps: Confirm an LPFR sample is ready for verification; confirm all mandatory hardware components and installation cables are available when needed; confirm the LPFR supports communication with the security element; confirm activation licenses are prepared if the LPFR is a software product.
ExpectedResult: The applicant can deliver an LPFR sample together with the required hardware, BE communication support, and activation licenses when applicable.
Notes: Extracted from the self-assessment section P1-P4 under Predmeti potrebni za rucno testiranje.

Title: Verify LPFR visual identification markings
Description: Verify that the LPFR visibly exposes the identification data required during visual verification.
Preconditions: An LPFR sample is available for inspection.
InputOrTrigger: Visual inspection of the submitted LPFR product.
Steps: Inspect the LPFR sample; for hardware products, verify that manufacturer, serial number, software version, and hardware version are visibly written on the product; for software products, verify that the same information is easily accessible.
ExpectedResult: The LPFR visibly exposes manufacturer, serial number, software version, and hardware version in the manner required for its product type.
Notes: Directly extracted from the Vizuelna verifikacija section.

Title: Provide SDC Analyzer test results for LPFR approval
Description: Verify that completed SDC Analyzer results are available as part of the LPFR approval package.
Preconditions: An LPFR approval process is in progress.
InputOrTrigger: Review of approval evidence for the submitted LPFR.
Steps: Collect the completed SDC Analyzer results; review that the requests were executed against the LPFR product during the approval process; confirm the results correspond to published technical-specification compliance testing.
ExpectedResult: Completed SDC Analyzer test results are available and demonstrate that LPFR functionality was tested against the published technical specification.
Notes: The source describes these results as part of the approval process and ties them directly to compliance testing.
